plans.title: Nifty 500 Intraday Momentum Portfolio — Per-Stock Tuned Vol-BO + CCRB
plans.subtitle: Two complementary intraday strategies (volume-breakout + CPR-compression-range-breakout) deployed as a per-stock-tuned portfolio. Each stock gets its own optimal variant (TF, vol_mult, gap, RSI, exit). 15 deployable stocks identified from top-100-by-turnover sweep so far; expanding to ~50-80 stocks via Nifty 500 wider universe to hit 2-3 trades/day target.

items.label: Strategy 1 — Volume-Breakout (vol-BO)
items.body: First 5/10/15-min candle of the session closes past prev_day_high (LONG) or prev_day_low (SHORT), with elevated volume. Most stocks favor either LONG or SHORT but rarely both. Origin: research/30 → /30b → /34.
pros: Simple trigger (1 candle close)
Fires once per day max per stock — easy to sequence
Cleanest direction-aware setup
cons: Per-stock variant tuning required (no single rule works broadly)
Strict promote gate (Sharpe>=0.5 + n>=15) only catches ~16% of liquid stocks

items.label: Strategy 2 — CPR-Compression Range Breakout (CCRB)
items.body: Today's CPR is narrow (volatility expectation low) AND yesterday had wide CPR (decisive move yesterday). When this setup hits, watch intraday for first 5/10/15-min close past prev_day_high (LONG) or prev_day_low (SHORT) between 09:20–14:00 IST. Origin: research/31 → /34. Variant grid trimmed (drop ctxN + ctxW_AND_N — both proved dead in liquid universe per research/32).

sections.title: Per-stock tuning — the deployable architecture
sections.body: There is NO single rule that works across many stocks. Best broad rules max out at avg Sharpe 0.12 (essentially noise). Per-stock tuning gives avg Sharpe 0.74 across the top-15 winners. The strategy fundamentally needs per-stock cells.
items: Each stock gets its own optimal variant: timeframe × vol_mult × gap_pct × RSI × exit_policy
Implemented as a STOCK_CONFIGS dict keyed by symbol; live scanner iterates and applies stock-specific rules
Adding/updating a stock = one dict entry change; no maintenance nightmare
Bucket-generic rules (gap+RSI premium / pure breakout) lose ~30% Sharpe — ruled out

sections.title: Top 15 vol-BO winners (Phase B done — top-100-by-turnover universe)
sections.body: Out of 119 stocks tested: 51 with Sharpe >= 0.3, 19 with Sharpe >= 0.5. Top 15 ranked by Sharpe:

items.label: 1. HAL — Sharpe 1.354
items.body: 5m short, vm 1.5x, gap-DOWN >= 0.5%, 5m RSI <= 40, exit T_NO. n=10, WR 90%, mean +1.41%.

items.label: 2. HDFCAMC — Sharpe 0.996
items.body: 10m long, vm 1.5x, gap-UP >= 0.5%, RSI >= 60, exit T_NO. n=11, WR 91%, mean +1.46%.

items.label: 3. RBLBANK — Sharpe 0.919
items.body: 10m long, vm 2.0x, any positive gap, no RSI, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=13, WR 92%, mean +1.08%.

items.label: 4. RELIANCE — Sharpe 0.881
items.body: 15m short, vm 3.0x, any negative gap, RSI <= 40, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=14, WR 93%, mean +1.17%.

items.label: 5. PERSISTENT — Sharpe 0.796
items.body: 5m long, vm 3.0x, no gap, no RSI, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=11, WR 82%, mean +1.42%.

items.label: 6. LAURUSLABS — Sharpe 0.772
items.body: 10m long, vm 2.0x, no gap, RSI >= 60, exit T_NO. n=13, WR 85%, mean +1.42%.

items.label: 7. COCHINSHIP — Sharpe 0.693
items.body: 15m short, vm 1.5x, any negative gap, no RSI, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=14, WR 79%, mean +0.70%.

items.label: 8. EICHERMOT — Sharpe 0.653
items.body: 5m long, vm 3.0x, no gap, no RSI, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=10, WR 80%, mean +0.50%.

items.label: 9. GODFRYPHLP — Sharpe 0.611
items.body: 10m long, vm 2.0x, any positive gap, no RSI, exit T_CHANDELIER_1.0. n=10, WR 80%, mean +1.88%. (User chart observation that triggered this whole research thread.)

items.label: 10. AARTIIND — Sharpe 0.603
items.body: 10m short, vm 2.0x, no gap, no RSI, exit T_NO. n=10, WR 80%, mean +1.94%.

items.label: 11. 3MINDIA — Sharpe 0.592
items.body: 5m short, vm 1.5x, no gap, no RSI, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=13, WR 85%, mean +0.60%.

items.label: 12. ACC — Sharpe 0.574
items.body: 5m short, vm 2.0x, no gap, no RSI, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=15, WR 87%, mean +0.85%.

items.label: 13. LT — Sharpe 0.574
items.body: 15m long, vm 2.0x, gap-UP >= 0.3%, RSI >= 60, exit T_R_TARGET_3R. n=11, WR 82%, mean +0.68%.

items.label: 14. WIPRO — Sharpe 0.572
items.body: 10m short, vm 2.0x, gap-DOWN >= 0.3%, RSI <= 40, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=12, WR 83%, mean +0.68%.

items.label: 15. ADANIGREEN — Sharpe 0.557
items.body: 15m short, vm 1.5x, gap-DOWN >= 0.3%, RSI <= 40, exit T_NO. n=11, WR 82%, mean +2.95%.

sections.title: Universal entry skeleton (per-stock parameters fill the blanks)
sections.body: All 15 vol-BO winners share the same trigger structure. Only the parameters differ per stock.
items: 1. Wait for the first 5/10/15-min candle of the session to close (TF varies per stock)
2. Direction-specific level break: LONG = close > prev_day_high; SHORT = close < prev_day_low
3. Volume confirm: bar volume >= vm × (20-day avg of that bar position) where vm in {1.5, 2.0, 3.0}
4. Optional gap rule: today_open vs prev_close — see per-stock spec
5. Optional RSI confirm: 5-min RSI(14) >= 60 (LONG) or <= 40 (SHORT)
6. If all gates pass: enter at candle's CLOSE price
7. R = abs(entry − first_bar_opposite_extreme)
8. Exit per stock-specific policy (T_NO time-only / T_R_TARGET_1R-3R / T_CHANDELIER trail)

sections.title: Position sizing (locked from ORB convention)
items: Risk per trade: Rs 3,000 (= 1% of effective trading capital)
Position size: floor(3000 / R) where R = SL distance per share
Max concurrent positions: 5 (matches existing ORB allocator)
Margin required: ~Rs 15-20 lakh deployed capital for 5 concurrent trades on Rs 3-4 lakh notional each

sections.title: Frequency analysis
sections.body: Top-15 portfolio at best-variant only fires ~84 trades/year = ~7/month = ~0.33/day. To reach 2-3 trades/day target (~500-750/year), need wider universe.
items: Current 15-stock portfolio: 84 trades/year
Top-19 (Sharpe >= 0.5): ~110/year
Top-51 (Sharpe >= 0.3): ~280/year
After Phase E expansion (Nifty 500 + Rs 50+ Cr/day liquidity): expected ~50-80 stocks at Sharpe >= 0.5 -> ~400-500 trades/year (~1.6-2.0/day)
Add top-2 cells per stock: ~600-800 trades/year (~2.4-3.2/day) — TARGET

sections.title: Phase plan (live status)
items: Phase A — Backfill 320 missing Nifty 500 stocks: DONE (380/395 succeeded; 15 lost to corporate-action delistings/renames)
Phase B — Vol-BO sweep on top-100 by turnover: DONE (230K signals, top 15 above)
Phase C — CCRB sweep on top-100 (trimmed grid): RUNNING (currently ~65/100 stocks after overnight resume)
Phase D — Aggregate Phase B+C, build cross-signal comparison: pending Phase C completion
Phase E — Backfill 135 new stocks (Rs 50+ Cr/day liquidity tier): pending; ~2h Kite API time
Phase F — Re-run vol-BO on 353-stock universe: pending; ~6h compute
Phase G — Re-run CCRB on 353-stock universe: pending; ~12h compute (overnight)
Phase H — Build final STOCK_CONFIGS dict; commit; ready for live paper scanner: pending

sections.title: Hard verdicts (already established)
items: Per-stock tuning beats single-rule and bucket-generic. avg Sharpe 0.74 vs 0.45-0.55 vs 0.12.
Banking + IT heavyweights (HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK, KOTAKBANK, TCS, INFY, HCLTECH) show NO clean intraday edge — too efficient.
Mid-caps with tight intraday range are the sweet spot — GODREJPROP, HAL, DELHIVERY, NESTLEIND, GRASIM, ULTRACEMCO etc.
Volume confirmation HELPS in every sweep that tested it (vm 2.0-3.0 cells beat off variants).
T_R_TARGET_1R is the most-common winning exit policy; T_STEP_TRAIL is consistently the worst (kills winners early).
Direction asymmetry is real per stock — argues for direction-aware deployment, not blind both-ways scanning.

sections.title: Crash recovery
sections.body: All work is in research/34_nifty500_expansion/ with MASTER_STATUS.md as authoritative resume point. Each phase script is resumable via skip-set on existing CSV. Heavy CSVs gitignored, scripts + small leaders.csv + MASTER_STATUS committed.
items: Master status: research/34_nifty500_expansion/MASTER_STATUS.md
Phase B results: research/34_nifty500_expansion/results/RESULTS.md (vol-BO findings)
Phase C in flight: research/34_nifty500_expansion/results/ccrb_run.log + ccrb_signals.csv (~700K rows)
Phase E plan: in MASTER_STATUS.md sections 5+
Existing stock leaderboards from prior runs: research/30b/results/volume_leaders.csv, research/31/results/ccrb_leaders.csv

plans.title: ORB Filter Tuning — Loosen RSI/Age/Drift To Reduce Missed Setups
plans.subtitle: Today (2026-04-27) BEL printed a clean SHORT breakdown that the code blocked all morning on the RSI ≤40 hard filter. RELIANCE entered 8 min late because the first eligible bar's 15-min RSI was 50.64 (needs ≥60). All four knobs are already in config.py:542-560 — RSI 60/40 was a baseline assumption (not swept), and signal_age=15min / signal_drift=0.5% were empirical guards added 2026-04-24 after the VEDL/TRENT stale-entry incident. None were formally backtested. This task sweeps them.

sections.title: Problems observed live on 2026-04-27
items: BEL — 14 SHORT signals fired between 09:33 and 10:53. Hard RSI filter (≤40) blocked all of them. RSI dropped to 38.52 at 10:43 (would have qualified) but signal_age guard had already expired the original breakout candle (43.5 min stale, max 15). Net: missed an entire clean SHORT setup.

RELIANCE — Entered 09:58:31 instead of ~09:53. First eligible 5-min bar (09:53:29) had 15-min RSI 50.64 < 60 threshold, blocked. Next bar (09:58:29) RSI 60.04, just barely qualified. ~5 min entry lag, plus higher entry price (1340.50 vs 1332.80).
VEDL — Multiple LONG signals all blocked by signal_drift (price moved >0.5% from breakout candle close after the gap-and-go). Anti-chase guard correct in principle, but missed a clean trend day.

sections.title: Decision — what NOT to change
items: RSI timeframe stays at 15-min (resampled from 5-min). Confirmed by user 2026-04-27 — do not switch to 5-min RSI.
Conviction-star thresholds (≤35 SHORT / ≥65 LONG) stay. Those are just for the A/B/C grade card, not a hard block — leave alone.
5-min bar-close confirmation stays. Strategy edge depends on it; intra-bar entries have worse expectancy.

sections.title: Sweep plan (run after market close)
sections.body: Backtest: research/20 universe (15 ORB stocks), 2024-01-01 to 2026-04-24 (full live data window plus paper history). Pass criteria: PF ≥ today's live PF, MaxDD ≤ today's, more trades than today (= fewer missed setups).

items.label: Phase 1 — Loosen RSI hard threshold + age guard (16 cells)
items.body: rsi_short_threshold ∈ {40 (current), 42, 45, 48} × signal_age_max_mins ∈ {15 (current), 20, 30, 45}. Mirror sweep on rsi_long_threshold ∈ {60 (current), 58, 55, 52} (4 cells).
pros: Directly addresses today's BEL miss + RELIANCE lag
Single-axis sweeps are easy to interpret
Tunes hard-block dials only — leaves star grading untouched
cons: RSI ≤45 SHORT may take more chop trades on weak setups
signal_age_max_mins=45 increases stale-entry risk if breakout has already extended

items.label: Phase 2 — Adjust signal_drift cap (4 cells)
items.body: signal_drift_max_pct ∈ {0.005 (current), 0.0085, 0.012, 0.015}. Only run if Phase 1 doesn't fix VEDL-style misses on its own.
pros: Catches gap-and-go runs after the breakout candle has already moved 0.5-1% past trigger
cons: Larger drift = paying up for entries; expected value erodes per backtest if too loose

sections.title: Where to make the change
items: Hard thresholds: services/orb_live_engine.py:1010-1023 (RSI), :1060-1089 (age/drift). All read from `cfg` at scan-time, defaults baked in via cfg.get(<key>, <default>).
Live config: config.py ORB_DEFAULTS dict — add `rsi_short_threshold`, `rsi_long_threshold`, `signal_age_max_mins`, `signal_drift_max_pct` keys explicitly with the chosen values.
Backtest harness: services/orb_backtest_engine.py + services/orb_daily_backtest.py — verify these accept the same cfg keys before sweeping.
Commits in two phases: (1) sweep results + verdict in research/2X folder, (2) config.py update applied AFTER sweep validates a winner.

sections.title: Estimated effort
items: Phase 1 sweep: ~30-45 min compute (16 cells + 4 LONG cells × ~1-2 min each on 15 symbols × ~500 trading days)
Phase 2 sweep: ~10-15 min if needed
Aggregation + decision: ~15 min review
Live config push: ~5 min (post-market only — no restart during 09:15-15:30 IST)

plans.title: F&O Multi-Signal Expansion — 3 Walk-Forward-Validated LONG Systems
plans.subtitle: Walk-forward PASSED on Donchian 55, ADX>30, RSI<40-pullback (LONG only). All 24 SHORT variants DOA. Spread-structure layer already deployed — winners auto-inherit bull-call generation. Wiring deferred — start observing paper signals first.

sections.title: Why this is parked, not deployed
sections.body: All three winners are LONG trend-continuation breakouts on the same 76-stock F&O universe that research/21 (252-day Donchian, already live as system_id='fno') already trades. Before adding 3 more system_ids and getting up to ~30 paper signals/day, we want a few weeks of data to measure per-day signal overlap with the live FNO system — if Donchian-55 fires on the same day as research/21 on 80% of trades, it's not a separate system, it's just an earlier trigger.

sections.title: What was validated (OOS = 2023-01-01 to 2025-12-31)
sections.body: Pass criteria: PF >= 1.20 AND Sharpe >= 0.8 AND MaxDD <= 30%.

items.label: Donchian 55 — 55_vol_3x_atr_floor (top pick)
items.body: PF 1.97, Sharpe 1.25, MaxDD 7.74%, CAGR +13.47%, n=175 OOS trades. Best risk-adjusted by a wide margin.
pros: Lowest OOS MaxDD across all winners
Tighter trigger than research/21's 252-day
Clean IS->OOS PF (1.88 -> 1.97)
cons: Likely to overlap heavily with research/21 — both are Donchian-style breakouts on same universe

items.label: ADX trend — adx_30 (highest CAGR)
items.body: PF 1.77, Sharpe 1.30, MaxDD 16.47%, CAGR +15.50%, n=225 OOS trades. Strict ADX>30 — different entry context than fresh breakouts.
pros: Highest OOS CAGR + Sharpe of all winners
Captures established trends rather than fresh breaks
Strong IS->OOS Sharpe lift (0.51 -> 1.30) suggests robustness
cons: adx_25_pure variant overfit badly (IS PF 2.57 -> OOS 1.22) — adx_30 only narrowly survived; small parameter shifts kill it

items.label: Pullback — pb_rsi40 (most non-correlated)
items.body: PF 1.55, Sharpe 1.01, MaxDD 18.19%, CAGR +12.99%, n=233 OOS trades. RSI<40 in established uptrend (50>200 SMA).
pros: Buy-the-dip vs breakout — most non-correlated entry style of the three
Largest OOS sample of the three
MACD/confluence variants underperformed — minimal-filter version won, lower overfit risk
cons: MaxDD 18% is highest of the three winners
RSI-driven mean-reversion-in-trend is more sensitive to whipsaw than pure breakout

sections.title: Hard verdicts from the sweep
items: 24 of 24 SHORT-direction variants failed (bearish breakdown 5, death cross 6, ADX-SHORT 5, pullback-SHORT 5, RS-low 3 implicit). F&O 76 universe is structurally long-biased — bear-put debit spreads from these signals lose money. Drop SHORT side entirely.
Confluence/extra-filter variants underperformed across the board — minimal-filter winners had higher OOS PF in 5 of 6 families. The 'every approach needs supporting filters' hypothesis didn't hold.

Second-tier passers (RS leadership rs_60d_z2.0, golden cross gc_ema_20_50) are deployable but RS overlaps heavily with Donchian 55 entry context, and gc_ema_20_50 has 24% MaxDD — close to the 30% gate. Skip these.
OOS regime (2023-25) was a strong Indian bull market. Bear-regime stress (2008-10, 2020-Q1) is the missing test before scaling capital.

sections.title: Deploy plan when we revisit
items: Step 1: Run signal-overlap analysis. For each of 3 winners, compute % of trade-days where it co-fires with research/21 fno system. If overlap > 60%, treat it as a filter on existing fno, not a new system.

Step 2: Add surviving signals as new system_ids in services/eod_breakout_scanner.py. Plumbing precedent: SYSTEM_NIFTY500/SMALLCAP/FNO already wired — same pattern. New ids: 'donchian55', 'adx30', 'pullback_rsi40'.

Step 3: Each new system_id auto-inherits the spread-structure layer (services/spread_structure.py builds bull-call on signal-fire, scanner persists JSON, EodBreakout.tsx renders strikes/debit/max-profit/RR + adjustment playbook).
Step 4: Paper-only for 30 trading days. Track per-system PF/Sharpe/DD against backtest expectations.
Step 5: Promote whichever survives the paper period to live with debit-spread sizing — risk = max_loss = net debit per spread.

sections.title: Artifacts
items: Status doc: docs/FNO-SPREAD-DEPLOY-STATUS.md (full result tables)
Per-signal folders: research/22_fno_bearish_breakdown/ through research/27_fno_pullback_trend/
Each contains: results/summary.csv (full sweep), results/walk_forward.csv (top variants), results/equity_*.csv, results/trades_<best>.csv, SIGNAL-STATUS.md (verdict)
Shared engine: research/22_fno_bearish_breakdown/scripts/_shared_engine.py (~430 lines, direction-aware, used by all 6 signal folders)

plans.title: Small/Micro-Cap Daily ATH/52w Breakout System
plans.subtitle: Walk-forward VALIDATED. OOS: PF 1.46 / Sharpe 1.43 / MaxDD 12.85% / CAGR +26.8% on 117 quality-filtered small/micro-caps. Sister to research/17.

sections.title: Pivot — daily bars, not intraday
sections.body: Earlier intraday version of this plan was BLOCKED by a data gap: local 5-min DB contains only Nifty 500 large/mid-caps, no genuine small/micro-cap 5-min bars. Pivoting to DAILY bars dodges the data problem entirely (we have 1,623 daily-bar symbols since 2018+) and aligns with the research/17 EOD scanner's winning structural pattern (PF 1.44 OOS / Sharpe 0.95 OOS on Nifty 500). This iteration applies the same family of rules to a smaller-cap universe with stricter quality filters.

items.label: Breakout trigger
items.body: Today's close > 252-day high (52-week breakout) OR today's close > all-time high in our data window. Both compute identically against rolling-max-excluding-today; use 252-day as primary since most stocks have <8 yrs in DB.

items.label: Volume confirmation
items.body: Today's volume >= 2.5x trailing 50-day average (stricter than research/17's 2.0x — small caps have noisier baseline volume, need higher conviction).

items.label: Regime filter
items.body: Today's close > 200-day SMA (only buy in confirmed uptrend on the stock itself; bear-market protection inherits same logic as research/17).

items.label: Execution timing
items.body: Signal triggers at EOD close. Enter at NEXT day's open (same operational pattern as research/17).

sections.title: Universe selection — quality filters before backtest

items.label: Step 1 — base universe
items.body: All daily-bar symbols in market_data_unified (~1,623). Filter to stocks with >=1500 daily bars since 2018-01-01. Exclude Nifty 500 (already covered by research/17) — focus on small/micro-cap exposure.

items.label: Step 2 — minimum market cap proxy via turnover
items.body: Min avg daily turnover Rs 5 Cr over trailing 60 days (liquidity floor for actual execution). Below this, even small position sizes face 0.5%+ impact cost.

items.label: Step 3 — maximum turnover cap (keep it small/micro)
items.body: Max avg daily turnover Rs 100 Cr over trailing 60 days — ensures we're not just re-testing mid-caps already in research/17 universe.

items.label: Step 4 — circuit / freeze filter (red flag check)
items.body: Exclude stocks where >5% of last 60 trading days hit a daily move >=10% (proxy for circuit hits / news-driven manipulation / illiquid pricing). These stocks have execution risk: positions get stuck when circuit triggers.

items.label: Step 5 — series filter
items.body: Keep only stocks in the 'EQ' (regular cash) series. Exclude T2T (trade-to-trade), Z series, BE series — these have settlement constraints incompatible with a swing entry/exit pattern.

items.label: Step 6 — volatility floor
items.body: Min avg ATR-as-pct-of-price >= 1% (exclude completely range-bound dead stocks).

items.label: Stop loss
items.body: max(entry - 2 * ATR(14), entry * 0.92) — whichever closer to entry. Caps day-1 risk at 8%.

items.label: Target — fixed 25%
items.body: Entry × 1.25. No trailing stop, no time stop except 60-day max-hold safety. Same logic as research/17 D1_fixed_25_8 (the OOS-validated winner).

items.label: Why fixed 25% (not trailing)
items.body: Research/17 walk-forward proved fixed targets dominate trailing stops on daily-bar momentum: 41-day avg hold lets winners run to full 25%, trail-based exits get whipped on retracements before the move completes.

items.label: Risk per trade
items.body: 1% of equity. Quantity = floor((equity * 0.01) / (entry - stop)).

items.label: Max concurrent positions
items.body: 10 (same as research/17). On Rs 10L capital, max Rs 1L notional per position.

items.label: Costs
items.body: 0.30% round-trip (vs research/17's 0.20% — small caps have wider spreads). Stress test at 0.50%.

items.label: Capital allocation
items.body: Rs 10,00,000 initial. Long-only (small-cap shorts via cash impossible; F&O futures unavailable on this universe). Skips short signal handling entirely.

sections.title: Backtest scope
sections.body: Period 2018-01-01 to 2025-12-31 (8 years). Bar TF: daily. Capital: Rs 10L. Walk-forward train 2018-2022 / test 2023-2025 (matches research/17 split for direct comparison).

sections.title: Pass criteria
items: OOS Profit Factor >= 1.20 (same as research/17)
OOS Sharpe >= 0.8
OOS MaxDD <= 30% (same as research/17 — small-cap MaxDD will likely be higher than mid-cap, but we accept that for the higher CAGR potential)
Trade frequency: 30-100 trades/year across the curated universe

sections.title: Why this should work (a-priori thesis)
sections.body: Research/17 proved daily ATH/52w breakouts + volume + regime filter is structurally edge-positive on Nifty 500. Small-caps amplify the move size when they break out (higher beta of single-stock momentum), but also amplify the noise (worse spreads, more news-driven false breakouts). The strict quality filters (turnover, circuit history, EQ series, volatility floor) should remove enough noise to leave the genuine edge. Net expectation: similar PF/Sharpe to research/17 (1.4 / 0.9), potentially higher CAGR, slightly higher MaxDD.

plans.title: ORB Directional Options — Nifty
plans.subtitle: 5-min directional options overlay on the existing ORB cash signal

sections.title: Goal
sections.body: When the ORB signal fires on any of the 15 cash stocks, mirror that directional view on NIFTY via options. Exploit the already-computed ORB conviction to size an options trade without taking separate directional risk on the index. Paper-only until backtest confirms edge.

items.label: Variant A · Pure naked option sell
items.body: On SHORT signal → SELL ATM or slightly-OTM NIFTY CE. On LONG signal → SELL ATM or slightly-OTM NIFTY PE.
pros: Collect full premium if direction is right
Simple 1-leg management
Maximum theta capture
cons: Unbounded risk on the wrong-side tail
Margin intensive

items.label: Variant B · Directional strangle
items.body: On SHORT signal → SELL closer-ATM CE + SELL far-OTM PE. On LONG signal → SELL closer-ATM PE + SELL far-OTM CE. Far-leg acts as tail hedge and improves margin.
pros: Covers both tails partially
Lower net margin via spread benefit
Extra premium from the hedge leg
cons: Two-leg management overhead
Caps upside vs naked

items.label: Variant C · Conviction-ranked router
items.body: Route grade A+/A signals to Variant A (naked — high conviction) · Route B/C to Variant B (hedged) · Skip anything with no stars.
pros: Uses existing conviction rubric already saved on positions
Right risk profile for each signal quality
cons: Adds complexity
Needs sample size to confirm grade-vs-outcome correlation

items.label: 1. Hard SL at 2× entry premium
items.body: Classic short-premium rule. Max loss = initial premium collected. Place as exchange SL-L with trigger at 2× entry, limit at 2.1× entry.

items.label: 2. SuperTrend trail on NIFTY spot
items.body: Exit the option when NIFTY spot SuperTrend flips against the trade. Test ST(7, 2.0) and ST(7, 3.0) — narrower band (2.0) catches smaller reversals but may whipsaw.

items.label: 3. Time-based exit
items.body: Candidates: 14:30 (aggressive) · 15:15 (standard EOD) · 15:20 (Zerodha MIS auto-squareoff). Test whether 14:30 loses the late-afternoon intrinsic decay edge.

items.label: 4. Partial scale-out
items.body: Mirror the ORB R-multiple style: exit 50% of lots at +30% premium decay (i.e. option value drops to 0.7× entry) · let remainder ride till SL or ST-flip.

sections.title: Open design questions
items: Strike selection: fixed-offset from spot? LTP-based search targeting premium ~Rs 80? Delta-based (e.g. short the 0.30 delta)?
Capital allocation: 1 lot per ORB signal? Scaled by conviction grade? Capped by current MIS margin headroom?
Concurrent cap: how many parallel option positions? 1? 3? 5?
Expiry choice: current-week for max theta + max gamma, or next-week for smoother decay?
SL placement: exchange-side SL-L on the option leg, or monitor-driven 2× check every tick?
Direction conflict: what if two ORB stocks give opposite signals? Take both (opposing NIFTY options), net out, or pick by conviction?

sections.title: Prerequisites before backtesting
items: Need 20+ trading days of captured 1-min options data (currently 3 sessions)
Reconstruct 5-min NIFTY candles from 1-min data for the ST calculation
Wire the ORB signal bus to emit signals to a second consumer (options engine)
Build a simple backtester using the captured option_chain snapshots

sections.title: Integration notes
items: Reuses the existing ORB signal engine 1:1 — no new signal logic
Fires at the same moment the cash ORB trade fires (so same filters apply)
Conviction grade already saved on orb_positions — direct lookup
New DB: backtest_data/orb_options.db (positions / orders / pnl)
Keep in paper mode (nas-style) until backtest shows edge

plans.title: Intraday 75%+ WR Three-System Portfolio — 5-min Equity (Long + Short)
plans.subtitle: Three walk-forward-validated systems on 67 unique mid-cap stocks. Short does drift-continuation when NIFTY is weak; Long-MR does mean-reversion bounce when only the stock is weak; Long-TC does trend-continuation pullback on gap-up days. Test WR 79–90%, MaxDD ≤ 6%. Paper-trade before scaling. (research/37 closed 2026-05-06.)

sections.title: Headline — three walk-forward-validated systems
sections.body: All three exit on TP 0.5% / SL 1.5% / EOD. RR is 1:3 against, but high WR (79–90%) drives positive expectancy. Out-of-sample period 2025-10-01 → 2026-03-25.

items.label: SYSTEM 1 — DIAMOND SHORT (drift continuation, weak market days)
items.body: 25-stock cohort: ZEEL, EDELWEISS, ASHOKA, CDSL, BANDHANBNK, KNRCON, RAIN, GMDCLTD, HEG, NATCOPHARM, SJVN, PRAJIND, TIINDIA, SUZLON, AMBER, RCF, NETWORK18, NAM-INDIA, BAYERCROP, TCIEXP, AARTIIND, NATIONALUM, IDEA, LTTS, NBCC. Variant B (volume): test WR 79.4%, n=310, PF 1.69, MaxDD 5.3% — ~705 trades/yr. Variant A (strict): test WR 85.3%, n=68, PF 2.08, MaxDD 2.5% — ~155 trades/yr.
pros: Highest trade frequency of the three (705/yr volume mode)
Mid-cap shorts have structural drift edge (per Stage 7 screen)
MaxDD bounded at 5–6%
cons: Short-only — needs SLB/MIS borrow availability
1:3 RR is psychologically uncomfortable; one SL eats 3 wins
NIFTY regime filter is single point of failure

items.label: SYSTEM 2 — LATE-DAY MEAN-REVERSION LONG (stock-weak / market-fine bounces)
items.body: 15-stock cohort (zero overlap with short): BALRAMCHIN, AUROPHARMA, DCBBANK, CYIENT, APLLTD, CERA, CGCL, BOSCHLTD, EIHOTEL, ASTRAL, CESC, CAPLIPOINT, AAVAS, ALKEM, APLAPOLLO. top10: test WR 80.6%, n=31, PF 2.58, MaxDD 1.7%, drift +1.8%. ~80 trades/yr. Triggers between 11:15–13:15 IST when stock down ≥2% but NIFTY not crashing further.
pros: Lowest test MaxDD of all three (1.7%)
Diversifies away from pure-short exposure
Mean-reversion edge well-documented in Indian mid-caps
cons: Smaller test sample (n=31) — needs paper validation
Low frequency (~80 trades/yr) — limited income on its own
Requires intraday RSI tracking + drop% calc

items.label: SYSTEM 3 — TREND-CONTINUATION PULLBACK LONG (gap-up + first-pullback)
items.body: 30-stock cohort (overlaps: AMBER, BANDHANBNK with short; APLAPOLLO with Long-MR): MARICO, FINEORG, CCL, ASAHIINDIA, GALAXYSURF, ASTRAZEN, JKPAPER, M&MFIN, JUBLFOOD, WHIRLPOOL, GODREJAGRO, INDIANB, ZYDUSWELL, CHOLAFIN, WELCORP, CUB, AMBER, INDIACEM, PNBHOUSING, HINDZINC, JKCEMENT, SOBHA, MGL, GRINDWELL, BAJFINANCE, DIXON, APLAPOLLO, DBL, BANDHANBNK, GODFRYPHLP. rank4 variant: test WR 82.4%, n=51, PF 3.11, drift +1.9%. ~110 trades/yr. Triggers in first 75 min on gap-up + pullback to VWAP.
pros: Best long-side test sample (n=51)
Highest test PF among the three (3.11)
Trend-following nature catches bull-run sessions the other two miss
cons: Highest cohort overlap (3 stocks shared)
First-hour-only window is narrow
Gap-up requirement (≥0.5%) skews toward bull regimes

items.label: SHORT entry — checked once at 09:45 IST (bar 6 close)
items.body: On each of the 25 short-cohort stocks: (1) NIFTY filter passes [Volume: NIFTY at 09:30 below today's 09:15 open, any amount; Strict: NIFTY ≥ 0.3% below today's open]. (2) Stock close (at 09:45) below its own intraday VWAP. (3) Stock RSI(14) < 40 (Volume) or < 35 (Strict). All true → SHORT at next bar's open.

items.label: LONG-MR entry — scanned continuously 11:15–13:15 IST (bars 24–48)
items.body: On each of the 15 long-MR-cohort stocks: (1) Stock down ≥ 2.0% from today's 09:15 open. (2) RSI(14) was below 28 in the past 6 bars. (3) RSI(14) now above 35. (4) Current 5-min bar bullish (close > open). (5) NIFTY not crashing — NIFTY's 5-min close not below NIFTY's own session VWAP at this bar. All true → LONG at next bar's open.

items.label: LONG-TC entry — scanned continuously 09:15–10:30 IST (bars 0–15)
items.body: On each of the 30 long-TC-cohort stocks: (1) Stock gapped up ≥ 0.5% (today's 09:15 open ≥ prev close × 1.005). (2) By bar 6, stock's session-high broke today's open by ≥ 0.5%. (3) Current bar's price within 0.3% of intraday VWAP (the pullback). (4) RSI(14) ≥ 45. (5) Current 5-min bar bullish. (6) NIFTY also gap-up + first bar bullish + above own VWAP at bar 6. All true → LONG at next bar's open.

items.label: Exit rules (all three systems share)
items.body: TP at +0.5% (long) / −0.5% (short). SL at −1.5% (long) / +1.5% (short). Force-exit at 15:25 IST if neither hit. Whichever fires first wins.

sections.title: Combined daily expected behaviour (3 systems together)
items: Volume mode (Short-Variant-B + LongMR-top10 + LongTC-rank4): ~895 trades/year, ~3.6 trades/session average. Capital rotates across regimes — short on weak-NIFTY days, long-TC on gap-up bullish days, long-MR on stock-specific weakness within fine markets.
Conviction mode (Short-Variant-A + LongMR-top5 + LongTC-rank4): ~305 trades/year, ~1.2 trades/session. Highest WR everywhere, lower frequency.

Same-day-same-stock conflict is structurally impossible — entry conditions are mutually exclusive (Short fires on weak+weak, Long-TC fires on gap-up+strong, Long-MR fires on stock-weak/NIFTY-not-crashing).
67 unique stocks across cohorts; only 3 stocks appear in two lists (AMBER, BANDHANBNK in both Short and Long-TC; APLAPOLLO in both Long-MR and Long-TC).

sections.title: Why the 3 mechanisms are different (not redundant)
items: SHORT exploits a structural intraday drift in mid-caps that open weak when the broader tape confirms — herd selling extends; stops cascade; no buyers come in.
LONG-MR exploits the inverse: when only one stock dumps but the index is fine, the move is usually idiosyncratic (algo, block, news) and snaps back as institutional buyers step in at oversold levels.
LONG-TC exploits classical trend continuation: a confirmed gap-up + bullish-NIFTY morning is a high-edge regime where the first pullback to VWAP is bought aggressively.
Together they cover the three intraday regimes that exist in this universe — broad weakness, lone weakness in healthy market, and confirmed bullish trend.

sections.title: Production checklist (before going live)
items: Re-confirm all three stock cohorts with 6-month rolling re-screen (cohorts may rotate as macro context shifts).
Add 0.15% per-trade slippage cost and re-run all three. The TP 0.5% target is small — slippage of 0.1% materially changes net economics.
Verify SLB/MIS borrow availability daily for short-cohort names — some (NBCC, KRBL-tier) have intermittent constraints.
Position sizing: per-trade risk ≤ 0.5% of account so a SL hit is bounded.
Concurrency cap: max 5–7 simultaneous positions across all three systems.
Codify NIFTY filter as a session-level gate: short filter at 09:45 IST, long-TC filter at 09:45 IST + per-bar check, long-MR filter at each scan bar.
Daily loss circuit-breaker: if 3 SLs hit on a single session, halt for the day.
Paper-trade for 2–3 weeks across all three, in mutually-respecting concurrency, before scaling capital.

sections.title: Open work / next steps
items: Stage 13 — combined 3-system portfolio backtest with realistic capital constraints, concurrency cap, unified equity curve. Bridges per-system stats to actual account-NAV behaviour. (Pending — script written but function-name mismatch needs reconciling against the 11b/11c sub-agent output before run.)
Trade journal app feature (separate plan) — to record daily P&L, signal-fires, fills, slippage. Will track this 3-system portfolio in production.
Paper-trade adapter to feed all 3 systems' signals into the existing app paper-mode infrastructure (similar to ORB paper / NAS paper).
